questions.question: What are natural numbers?
questions.explanation: Natural numbers are used for counting objects and start from 1.

questions.question: What is the difference between natural numbers and whole numbers?
questions.correct_answer: Whole numbers include zero but natural numbers do not
questions.explanation: Whole numbers are {0,1,2,3,...} while natural numbers start from 1.

questions.question: What are integers?
questions.correct_answer: Numbers that include negative numbers, zero, and positive numbers

questions.question: What is a rational number?
questions.correct_answer: A number that can be written in the form p/q where q ≠ 0
questions.explanation: Both numerator and denominator must be integers and denominator cannot be zero.

questions.question: What type of decimal expansion do rational numbers have?
questions.explanation: When fractions are converted into decimals they either terminate or repeat.

questions.question: What is an irrational number?
questions.correct_answer: A number that cannot be written in the form p/q
questions.explanation: Its decimal expansion is non-terminating and non-repeating.

questions.question: Give examples of irrational numbers.
questions.explanation: These numbers cannot be expressed as fractions.

questions.question: What are real numbers?
questions.correct_answer: All rational and irrational numbers together
questions.explanation: Every point on the number line represents a real number.

questions.question: What happens when a rational number is added to an irrational number?
questions.explanation: The irrational part cannot be cancelled by a rational number.

questions.question: What type of decimal expansion do irrational numbers have?
questions.explanation: The digits continue infinitely without repeating.

notes.title: Natural Numbers
notes.content: Natural numbers are counting numbers starting from 1 and continuing infinitely.

notes.title: Rational Numbers
notes.content: Rational numbers can be expressed as p/q where p and q are integers and q ≠ 0.

notes.title: Irrational Numbers
notes.content: Irrational numbers have non-terminating and non-repeating decimal expansions.

notes.title: Real Numbers
notes.content: Real numbers include both rational and irrational numbers and are represented on the number line.

notes.title: Decimal Expansions
notes.content: Rational numbers have terminating or recurring decimals, while irrational numbers have non-repeating decimals.

facts.fact: Natural numbers start from 1 while whole numbers start from 0.
Every integer is also a rational number because it can be written as p/1.
Between any two rational numbers there are infinitely many rational numbers.
Every point on the number line represents a real number.
